metadata.title: Tamil Nadu Government Schemes Dataset
metadata.description: A comprehensive dataset of government schemes launched and active in Tamil Nadu, covering social welfare, education, health, agriculture, women empowerment, housing, and more.
source_references: TNPSC Thervupettagam – Recent TN Government Schemes
PRS India – Tamil Nadu Budget Analysis 2024-25

schemes.name: Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam (Women's Rights Grant Scheme)
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare & Women Empowerment Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Provide direct monthly financial assistance of ₹1,000 to women heads of households to improve livelihoods, promote gender equality, and ensure economic independence.
schemes.beneficiaries: Women recognized as heads of households on family ration cards; widows, unmarried single women, and trans women running households.
eligibility: Age: Must have completed 21 years (born before Sept 15, 2002)
Annual household income less than ₹2.5 lakhs
Own less than 5 acres of wetland or 10 acres of dryland
Domestic electricity consumption less than 3,600 units per year

benefits.mode: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to bank account

schemes.coverage: 10.6 million eligible women across Tamil Nadu
schemes.notes: Launched on the birthday of C.N. Annadurai; no separate income certificate required.

schemes.name: Pudhumai Penn Thittam (Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Higher Education Assurance Scheme)
schemes.implementing_department: School Education Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Encourage girl students to continue higher education by providing monthly financial assistance.
schemes.beneficiaries: Girl students studying in government schools from Classes 6 to 12.
eligibility: Must be enrolled in a government school (Class 6–12)
Must have studied from Class 1 in a government school

benefits.mode: DBT to student or guardian's bank account

schemes.coverage: All eligible girl students in Tamil Nadu government schools
schemes.notes: One of the largest education support schemes for girls in Tamil Nadu.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare & Women Empowerment Department
schemes.objective: Provide free nutritious breakfast to primary school students to improve attendance and reduce malnutrition.
schemes.beneficiaries: Students of Classes 1 to 5 in government primary schools across Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Enrolled in Classes 1–5 in any government school in Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Free nutritious breakfast on all school days

schemes.name: Naan Mudhalvan Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation (TNSDC)
schemes.objective: Transform students and youth into industry-ready professionals by providing skill training, career counselling, and competitive exam preparation.
schemes.beneficiaries: School and college students, and youth of Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Students in government and government-aided schools, colleges, and universities
Age 18–29 for competitive exam training
Bachelor's degree for SSC/IBPS/Railways training track

benefits.type: Free skill training, career guidance, competitive exam coaching
benefits.special_provision: 6-month residential training with food and accommodation for 1,000 selected students (2024-25 budget)

schemes.notes: Includes SIRPI initiative for police-public collaboration and youth leadership.

schemes.name: Vetri Nichayam Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation (TNSDC)
schemes.objective: Provide free industry-relevant skill training to youth to enhance employability.
schemes.beneficiaries: Youth aged 18–35 years across Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Age between 18 and 35 years
Resident of Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Free skill training in various domains including IT, manufacturing, services, and trades

schemes.name: Green Tamil Nadu Mission
schemes.implementing_department: Environment, Climate Change and Forest Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Increase forest and tree cover of Tamil Nadu to 33% of the state's land area through large-scale tree-planting and ecological restoration.
schemes.beneficiaries: All citizens of Tamil Nadu; special benefits for tribals, farmers, and rural communities.
eligibility: Open to all government departments, NGOs, private organizations, educational institutions, and communities

benefits.type: Ecological restoration, biodiversity enhancement, economic income from high-value trees
benefits.technology: Geo-tagging and digital monitoring via greentnmission.com

schemes.notes: Inaugurated at Vandalur, Chengalpattu; partnerships with Hyundai India Motors and others.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Thayumanuvar Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Department of Rural Development and Panchayat Raj
schemes.objective: Uplift approximately 5 lakh extremely poor families from poverty within 2 years by integrating all forms of government assistance.
schemes.beneficiaries: Destitute persons, elderly living alone, single-parent families, orphaned children, mentally handicapped individuals, and those in financial distress.
eligibility: Identified as extremely poor per NITI Aayog criteria (approx. 2.2% of state population)
Permanent resident of Tamil Nadu
Must belong to destitute, elderly alone, single-parent, or disabled category

benefits.type: Comprehensive bundled support – financial aid, social welfare, skill development, and basic amenities

schemes.name: Mission Nature Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu School Education Department (in collaboration with WWF India)
schemes.objective: Improve environmental awareness among students through practical projects in schools, homes, and communities.
schemes.beneficiaries: Students from 6,029 government schools with advanced technology laboratories.
eligibility: Government schools equipped with advanced technology laboratories
Each school selects 5 participating students

benefits.type: Environmental education, hands-on projects, awards for best schools and students
benefits.awards: Top 5 schools and 25 students recognized and rewarded

schemes.name: Tamizh Pudhalvan Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: School Education Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Provide financial assistance to boys from government schools for purchasing learning materials during higher education.
schemes.beneficiaries: Male students who have studied in government schools from Class 6 to Class 12.
eligibility: Must have studied in government schools from Class 6 to 12
Must be pursuing higher education

benefits.purpose: Purchase of learning materials for higher education

schemes.coverage: Approximately 3 lakh students; allocation of ₹360 crore in 2024-25

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS)
schemes.implementing_department: Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Provide free medical treatment to economically weaker sections covering major surgeries and hospital expenses.
schemes.beneficiaries: Families with annual income below ₹72,000 holding valid ration cards.
eligibility: Annual income below ₹72,000
Must hold a valid government ration card

benefits.type: Cashless treatment at empanelled government and private hospitals
benefits.procedures_covered: Over 1,400 procedures including heart surgery, cancer treatment, organ transplants

schemes.notes: One of the largest health insurance schemes in India.

schemes.name: Nalam Kakkum Stalin Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Health and Family Welfare Department
schemes.objective: Ensure quality healthcare reaches every corner of Tamil Nadu through large-scale health camps and mobile healthcare units.
schemes.beneficiaries: All citizens of Tamil Nadu, with focus on rural and remote areas.
eligibility: Open to all residents of Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Free health screening, diagnosis, medicines, and referrals through mobile and camp-based healthcare

schemes.coverage: Statewide, targeting rural and underserved communities

schemes.name: 108 Emergency Response Management Service
schemes.implementing_department: Health and Family Welfare Department (via EMRI Hyderabad MoU)
schemes.objective: Provide integrated emergency response services (medical, police, and fire) through a single toll-free number.
schemes.beneficiaries: All citizens of Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Open to all residents and visitors in Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Free ambulance services, emergency medical care, coordination with police and fire services

schemes.name: Kalaignarin Kanavu Illam (Housing Scheme)
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Housing Board / Rural Development Department
schemes.objective: Construct 8 lakh concrete houses in rural areas by 2030; provide funds to selected beneficiaries for house construction.
schemes.beneficiaries: Below poverty line families in rural Tamil Nadu without permanent housing.
eligibility: BPL family in rural Tamil Nadu
No existing permanent house
Selected beneficiary under the scheme

schemes.name: Pudhumai Penn Higher Education Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Higher Education Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Encourage girls who have completed Class 12 in government schools to pursue higher education by providing monthly stipend.
schemes.beneficiaries: Girl students pursuing higher education (college) after completing government school education.
eligibility: Should have completed Classes 1–12 in government schools
Currently enrolled in a college/university
Tamil Nadu resident

schemes.name: Old Age Pension Scheme (Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme – TN component)
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department
schemes.objective: Provide monthly financial assistance to senior citizens living below the poverty line.
schemes.beneficiaries: Senior citizens aged 60 and above belonging to BPL families.
eligibility: Age 60 years and above
BPL card holder or annual income below threshold
Permanent resident of Tamil Nadu

schemes.name: Widow Pension Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department
schemes.objective: Provide monthly financial support to widows without adequate income for their livelihood.
schemes.beneficiaries: Widows residing in Tamil Nadu without sufficient income.
eligibility: Widow status
Annual income below prescribed limit
Permanent resident of Tamil Nadu

schemes.name: Differently-Abled Pension Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department
schemes.objective: Provide monthly pension to persons with disabilities for their basic financial security.
schemes.beneficiaries: Persons with 40% or more disability in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Minimum 40% disability certified by government medical board
Annual income below prescribed limit
Tamil Nadu resident

schemes.name: Dr. Muthulakshmi Reddy Maternity Benefit Scheme
schemes.local_name: டாக்டர் முத்துலட்சுமி ரெட்டி மகப்பேறு உதவி திட்டம்
schemes.implementing_department: Health and Family Welfare Department
schemes.objective: Provide financial assistance to pregnant women to ensure better nutrition and maternal healthcare.
schemes.beneficiaries: Pregnant women belonging to BPL or low-income families in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Registered at government health centre or hospital
BPL or low-income family
Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.installments: Paid in installments during pregnancy and post-delivery

schemes.name: Cradle Baby Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Health and Family Welfare Department / Social Welfare Department
schemes.objective: Prevent female infanticide by allowing parents to safely surrender unwanted girl children at designated cradle centres.
schemes.beneficiaries: Unwanted/abandoned girl children; families in distress.
eligibility: Open to any family wishing to surrender a girl child
No questions asked policy

benefits.type: Safe shelter, adoption facilitation, free care for surrendered girl children

schemes.name: Girl Child Protection Scheme (Annakkal Scheme)
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department
schemes.objective: Encourage families to value the girl child by providing a fixed deposit to ensure financial security upon adulthood.
schemes.beneficiaries: Girl children born on or after August 1, 2011 in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Girl child born on or after 01/08/2011
Family with one girl child: ₹50,000 FD
Family with two girl children: ₹25,000 each
Parent must have undergone sterilization
Application before child turns 3 years old
Parents/grandparents domiciled in Tamil Nadu for 10 years

benefits.deposited_with: Tamil Nadu Power Finance and Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited

schemes.coverage: Statewide; extended to Sri Lankan Tamil refugees in camps

schemes.name: Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Ninaivu Marriage Assistance Scheme
schemes.local_name: மூவலூர் ராமாமிர்தம் அம்மையார் நினைவு திருமண உதவி திட்டம்
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department
schemes.objective: Provide financial assistance for the purchase of marriage ornaments (Thaali/mangalsutra) to prevent dowry harassment and child marriages.
schemes.beneficiaries: Brides from economically weaker families in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Bride must have completed 18 years of age
Must be from BPL or low-income family
Application must be submitted before the marriage date
Must hold diploma or higher qualification (for enhanced benefit)

benefits.type: One-time grant for gold thaali purchase

schemes.name: Dr. Dharmambal Ammaiyar Ninaivu Widow Remarriage Assistance Scheme
schemes.local_name: டாக்டர் தர்மாம்பாள் அம்மையார் நினைவு விதவை மறுமணம் உதவி திட்டம்
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department
schemes.objective: Encourage widow remarriage and provide financial support for rehabilitation.
schemes.beneficiaries: Widows in Tamil Nadu who wish to remarry.
eligibility: Widow residing in Tamil Nadu
Intending to remarry
Application before or on marriage date

benefits.type: One-time grant

schemes.name: Tholkudi Tribal Development Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
schemes.objective: Develop tribal communities by establishing basic infrastructure and providing educational and training opportunities.
schemes.beneficiaries: Tribal students and communities in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Tribal communities and students in designated tribal areas
Students seeking higher education in engineering, medicine, and other fields
Students seeking study-abroad opportunities

benefits.type: Infrastructure development, educational support, special classes, study-abroad opportunities

schemes.notes: Inauguration ceremony held at Namakkal in March 2024.

schemes.name: Free Bus Travel for Women Scheme (Hills Areas)
schemes.local_name: மலைப் பகுதி மகளிர் இலவச பேருந்து பயண திட்டம்
schemes.implementing_department: Transport Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Enhance mobility and empowerment of women in hilly areas by providing free bus travel.
schemes.beneficiaries: Women, including college students, in Nilgiris, Kodaikanal, Valparai, Yercaud, and Karumandurai hilly areas.
eligibility: Women residents of specified hilly areas
Covers 65 villages in Yercaud Hills and 98 villages in Karumandurai Hills

benefits.type: Free bus travel on government buses within specified hilly areas

schemes.name: Imaigal Scheme (Girl Child Protection – Police)
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Police – Northern Zone (under IG Kannan)
schemes.objective: Protect girl children from sexual crimes through a dedicated operational framework within the Tamil Nadu Police.
schemes.beneficiaries: Girl children in Chennai, Kanchipuram, Chengalpattu, and Vellore districts.
eligibility: All girl children in the Northern Zone police jurisdiction

benefits.type: Legal support, case registration, charge sheet within 60 days, victim compensation and rehabilitation
benefits.collaboration: Engages multiple government departments for coordinated response

schemes.name: Neengal Nalama Scheme (Citizens' Feedback on Welfare Schemes)
schemes.implementing_department: Chief Minister's Office, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Bridge the gap between government and public by gathering direct feedback on welfare schemes to improve delivery and reach.
schemes.beneficiaries: All citizens of Tamil Nadu who are beneficiaries of welfare schemes.
eligibility: Any resident of Tamil Nadu who is a beneficiary of state welfare schemes

benefits.type: Improved public service delivery; feedback mechanism to enhance scheme effectiveness

schemes.name: You Are Your Own King Scheme (Nee Nee Rajave / Traditional Arts Revival)
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation
schemes.objective: Empower youth by revitalizing traditional arts, promoting skill development, and facilitating employment opportunities.
schemes.beneficiaries: Youth interested in traditional arts and crafts across Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Youth passionate about traditional Tamil arts and crafts
Open to all regions of Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Specialized skill training in woodwork, Thanjavur dolls/painting, palm leaf art, sea shell art, clay art, pottery, handloom dyeing, Pathamadai mat making, etc.

schemes.notes: Implemented under leadership of Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin.

schemes.name: SIRPI Initiative (Student-Police Partnership)
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Police Department
schemes.objective: Create responsible students who will contribute to a better society by bridging the gap between police and public.
schemes.beneficiaries: School and college students across Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Students in government and government-aided schools and colleges

benefits.type: Leadership training, mentoring by police officers, anti-crime awareness, skills guidance

schemes.name: Thagaisal Schools / Model Schools Initiative
schemes.implementing_department: School Education Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Transform government schools to match private school standards with modern facilities and holistic education.
schemes.beneficiaries: Students in selected government schools across Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Students enrolled in selected model/thagaisal government schools

benefits.type: Modern computer labs, advanced science labs, integrated libraries, sports and extracurricular facilities, adequate teachers

schemes.notes: Inaugurated jointly by CM M.K. Stalin and Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal.

schemes.name: Valar 4.0 Portal for MSMEs and Entrepreneurs
schemes.implementing_department: Department of Information Technology and Digital Services; Tamil Nadu e-Governance Agency (TNeGA)
schemes.objective: Support entrepreneurs and MSMEs by enhancing capabilities, productivity, technology adoption, and idea exchange to achieve a USD 1 trillion economy target.
schemes.beneficiaries: Entrepreneurs and MSME owners in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Entrepreneurs and industries operating in Tamil Nadu
MSMEs registered under relevant government schemes

benefits.type: Online support portal, issue resolution, capacity building, IIT-Madras collaboration

schemes.name: Free Electricity for Agriculture Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Agriculture Department / TANGEDCO
schemes.objective: Provide free electricity to farmers for agricultural pump sets to reduce input costs.
schemes.beneficiaries: Farmers with agricultural pump sets connected to the electricity grid.
eligibility: Registered farmers in Tamil Nadu
Must have legal pump set connection for agriculture

benefits.type: Free electricity for agricultural pump sets (upto specified HP)

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu Crop Insurance Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Agriculture Department / Cooperation Department
schemes.objective: Protect farmers from financial loss due to crop damage caused by natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
schemes.beneficiaries: Farmers growing notified crops in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Farmers (loanee and non-loanee) growing notified crops
Tamil Nadu resident
Must enroll before sowing season deadline

benefits.type: Compensation for crop loss; premium subsidized by state and central government

schemes.notes: Implemented under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana framework.

schemes.name: Agricultural Input Subsidy Scheme (Seeds, Fertilizers)
schemes.implementing_department: Agriculture Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Reduce the financial burden on farmers by providing subsidized seeds, fertilizers, and other agricultural inputs.
schemes.beneficiaries: Farmers in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Registered farmers in Tamil Nadu
Small and marginal farmers prioritized

benefits.type: Subsidized seeds, fertilizers, bio-inputs, and farm machinery

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu Skill Development Mission (TNSDC)
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Skill Development Corporation (TNSDC)
schemes.objective: Provide skill training to youth to enhance their employability and support the growth of the skilled workforce in Tamil Nadu.
schemes.beneficiaries: Youth aged 18–35 across Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.type: Free or subsidized skill training in multiple domains; placement assistance

schemes.name: Mahalir Thittam (Women Self-Help Group Scheme)
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Corporation for Development of Women (TNCDW)
schemes.objective: Empower rural women economically and socially through self-help groups (SHGs) by providing access to credit, training, and market linkages.
schemes.beneficiaries: Rural women across Tamil Nadu organized into SHGs.
eligibility: Rural women aged 18 and above
Group formation of 10–20 women
Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.type: Credit linkage, revolving fund, skill training, market access, entrepreneurship support

schemes.notes: Has significantly improved livelihoods of millions of women.

schemes.name: Free Laptop / Tablet Scheme for College Students
schemes.implementing_department: School Education / Higher Education Department
schemes.objective: Provide free laptops or tablets to government school and college students to enhance digital literacy and learning.
schemes.beneficiaries: Students in government colleges; government school students (Classes 6–8 for tablets).
eligibility: Students enrolled in government-aided colleges
Students in Classes 6–8 for tablet distribution
Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.type: Free laptop or tablet
benefits.latest_target: 20 lakh students to receive laptops/tablets over 2 years under 2025 announcement

schemes.notes: 2025 update: Free Laptop Scheme 2025 announced for 20 lakh students; distribution expected Feb–March 2026.

schemes.name: RTE Tamil Nadu (Right to Education – 25% Reservation)
schemes.implementing_department: School Education Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Ensure free and compulsory education to children from weaker sections through 25% reservation in private unaided schools.
schemes.beneficiaries: Children from economically weaker sections (EWS) and disadvantaged groups aged 6–14.
eligibility: Children aged 6–14 years
Must belong to EWS / disadvantaged groups as per defined criteria
Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.type: 25% reservation in private unaided schools; free education up to Class 8

schemes.name: Kalaignarin Kanavu Illam – Urban Housing Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Housing Board
schemes.objective: Provide affordable housing options to low and middle-income families in urban Tamil Nadu.
schemes.beneficiaries: Urban poor and lower-middle-income families.
eligibility: Urban residents of Tamil Nadu
Low to middle income group
No other pucca house in Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Subsidized housing units or financial assistance for house construction

schemes.coverage: Major urban centres in Tamil Nadu

schemes.name: Ungaludan Stalin Camp
schemes.implementing_department: Chief Minister's Office, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Bring government services directly to people and quickly resolve common problems through large-scale district camps.
schemes.beneficiaries: All citizens of Tamil Nadu in urban and rural areas.
eligibility: Any resident of Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: On-the-spot resolution of grievances, distribution of scheme benefits, multi-department services

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Kakkum Karangal for Ex-Servicemen
schemes.implementing_department: Ex-Servicemen Welfare Department / Chief Minister's Office
schemes.objective: Help retired defence personnel set up their own businesses through loans and financial assistance.
schemes.beneficiaries: Ex-servicemen and their families in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Retired defence personnel residing in Tamil Nadu
Family members of ex-servicemen

benefits.type: Subsidized loans and financial assistance for entrepreneurship and self-employment

schemes.name: MGNREGA Tamil Nadu (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)
schemes.implementing_department: Rural Development and Panchayat Raj Department
schemes.objective: Guarantee 100 days of wage employment per year to rural households whose adult members are willing to do unskilled manual work.
schemes.beneficiaries: Rural households in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Rural household in Tamil Nadu
Must hold a Job Card (MGNREGA Job Card)
Adult member willing to do unskilled manual work
schemes.coverage: All rural areas of Tamil Nadu
schemes.notes: Job Card List 2025-26 released; check at nrega.nic.in.

schemes.name: Aavin Milk Subsidy Card Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Cooperative Milk Producers Federation Limited (TCMPF – Aavin)
schemes.objective: Provide subsidized milk to families across Tamil Nadu to improve nutrition.
schemes.beneficiaries: Families registered with the Aavin milk card scheme.
eligibility: Any resident of Tamil Nadu
Can register online at aavin.tn.gov.in from January 2023

benefits.type: Subsidized or free milk supply through Aavin network

schemes.name: Inter-Caste Marriage Assistance Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department
schemes.objective: Promote social harmony by providing financial incentives to inter-caste married couples.
schemes.beneficiaries: Couples where one partner belongs to SC/ST category and the other to a different caste.
eligibility: One partner from SC/ST community
First marriage for both
Application within one year of marriage
Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.type: One-time cash grant

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu Backward Classes Higher Education Loan Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Backward Classes, Most Backward Classes and Minorities Welfare Department
schemes.objective: Enable students from backward classes to access higher education through subsidized loans.
schemes.beneficiaries: Students from BC/MBC/Minority communities pursuing higher education.
eligibility: Belongs to BC/MBC/Minority category
Admitted to recognized institution for higher education
Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.type: Low-interest or subsidized education loan

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu Noon Meal Scheme (Midday Meal Programme)
schemes.implementing_department: School Education Department / Social Welfare Department
schemes.objective: Provide free nutritious midday meals to school students to improve enrollment, attendance, and nutrition.
schemes.beneficiaries: Students in Classes 1 to 12 in government and government-aided schools.
eligibility: Enrolled in government or government-aided school in Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Free midday meal on all school days

schemes.notes: Pioneer scheme introduced by Chief Minister M.G. Ramachandran (MGR) in 1982.

schemes.name: Adi Dravidar Welfare Scholarships
schemes.implementing_department: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
schemes.objective: Support education of students from Scheduled Caste (SC) communities through scholarships and other assistance.
schemes.beneficiaries: Students from Scheduled Caste communities in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Belongs to SC community in Tamil Nadu
Enrolled in school or college
Income below prescribed limit

benefits.type: Pre-matric and post-matric scholarships, free hostel facilities, free coaching

schemes.name: Kalaignarin Kanavu Illam – Slum Clearance and Resettlement
schemes.implementing_department: Tamil Nadu Slum Clearance Board
schemes.objective: Resettle slum dwellers into permanent housing colonies with basic amenities.
schemes.beneficiaries: Slum dwellers in urban Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Residing in notified slum area
Urban resident of Tamil Nadu

benefits.type: Permanent tenement with water supply, sanitation, and electricity

schemes.name: Full-Time Ph.D Scholar Incentive Scheme
schemes.local_name: முழுநேர முனைவர் பட்ட ஆய்வாளர் ஊக்கத் திட்டம்
schemes.implementing_department: Higher Education Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
schemes.objective: Encourage research by providing financial incentives to full-time Ph.D scholars in Tamil Nadu universities.
schemes.beneficiaries: Full-time Ph.D scholars in Tamil Nadu state universities.
eligibility: Full-time Ph.D registration in a Tamil Nadu state university
Fresh application for 2024-25 released

benefits.type: Monthly research fellowship/incentive

schemes.name: Tamil Nadu Public Distribution System (PDS) – Free Rice Scheme
schemes.implementing_department: Civil Supplies and Consumer Protection Department
schemes.objective: Ensure food security for BPL and priority households by providing free rice through ration shops.
schemes.beneficiaries: Priority Household (PHH) and Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) cardholders in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Holds a valid ration card (PHH or AAY)
Tamil Nadu resident

benefits.type: Free rice per family unit per month

schemes.name: Education Loan Scheme for College Students (Budget 2024-25)
schemes.implementing_department: Higher Education Department / Banks
schemes.objective: Ensure adequate credit availability for college students pursuing higher education by facilitating bank education loans.
schemes.beneficiaries: College students in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Enrolled in a recognized college in Tamil Nadu
Tamil Nadu resident
schemes.notes: Announced in Tamil Nadu Budget 2024-25; disbursed through banks.
